summary: A solid soundboard capture (with some rough audience patches) highlighted by a scorching Black Peter and a tight Lazy Lightning > Supplication.
review: The October‑1977 Manor Downs show delivers classic Dead magic – blazing Black Peter, an epic Sugar Magnolia, a fire‑breathing The Other One and a razor‑sharp Lazy Lightning > Supplication.  The SBD source is clean, though the audience fill‑ins are noticeably thin, which some listeners flag as a drawback.  Overall the performance shines despite the cold night and occasional recording hiccups.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ≈ 4.2, with most comments praising the musical moments while noting only minor recording flaws; strong consensus yields a confident positive score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: SBD tracks are clear and well‑balanced; audience patches used to fill gaps are described as “crummy” or “terrible,” creating occasional drop‑outs but not overwhelming the overall listenability.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Cold, rowdy Texas night with a rodeo backdrop; the first set builds to a fiery Lazy Lightning > Supplication closing, while the second set ramps up with an ultra‑hot Sam & D segment and a soaring Black Peter.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead guitar is described as “shredding” and delivering hot solos (especially on Candyman and The Other One).
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh gets kudos for “dropping nukes” at the start of The Other One; his vocals are noted as hoarse but enthusiastic.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocal performance shines on Lightning > Supplication, with reviewers calling it full‑displayed prowess.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (or Brent) is praised for “angular key work” that adds richness and dialog to the set.
